goblin_shaman.name: goblin shaman
goblin_shaman.lore: Goblin shamans wield crude but effective tribal magic, channeling the malevolent will of their dark patron into hexes and curses. They hang back in combat, directing their kin with screeching commands. Their ritual masks are carved from dungeon mushroom caps soaked in alchemical reagents.

goblin_king.name: goblin king
goblin_king.lore: A goblin king rules through a combination of violence, cunning, and sheer volume. They are always larger than their subjects — sometimes naturally, sometimes through magical augmentation from their shaman advisors. Goblin kings wear crowns assembled from bent copper coins and teeth taken from rivals.

hobgoblin_captain.name: hobgoblin captain
hobgoblin_captain.lore: Hobgoblin captains have earned their rank through battlefield promotions in brutal campaigns. They carry unit banners that inspire nearby hobgoblins to fight with greater discipline. Their tactical acumen is impressive — they will always attempt to flank and cut off retreat before engaging.

hobgoblin_warlord.name: hobgoblin warlord
hobgoblin_warlord.lore: A hobgoblin warlord has survived dozens of battles through superior strategy and personal ferocity. They command absolute loyalty from their troops through a code of martial honor that punishes cowardice with death. The war trophies on their armor represent the most dangerous opponents they have personally defeated.

bugbear.name: bugbear
bugbear.lore: Bugbears are the largest of the common goblinoids, standing seven feet of muscle and fur. They are ambush predators who delight in causing fear before striking. Their natural stealth is uncanny for creatures their size — the silence before a bugbear attack is its only warning.

bugbear_chieftain.name: bugbear chieftain
bugbear_chieftain.lore: A bugbear chieftain has defeated every other bugbear in its territory. They rule with casual brutality, expecting tribute from lesser monsters in their vicinity. Their war trophies include the skulls of previous chieftains — a grim decoration that communicates their dominance without words.

orc_berserker.name: orc berserker
orc_berserker.lore: Orc berserkers enter a blood rage in combat, attacking with total disregard for their own safety. They feel no pain while berserking — wounds that would drop a normal warrior only fuel their fury. Their warband sends them in first as a shock unit, then follows up while the enemy is disrupted.

orc_warchief.name: orc warchief
orc_warchief.lore: An orc warchief commands multiple warbands and holds territory through sheer personal power. They are chosen not by birth but by combat — any orc can challenge a warchief, though few survive the attempt. Their strategems are surprisingly sophisticated for creatures who view battlefield elegance as weakness.

bandit_captain.name: bandit captain
bandit_captain.lore: Bandit captains are experienced fighters who have chosen outlawry over legitimate service. They lead small bands of armed robbers and claim dungeon sections as toll roads. Their combat style is practical and ruthless — they target the most dangerous enemy first and show no mercy.

dark_cultist.name: dark cultist
dark_cultist.lore: Dark cultists have abandoned civilized life to serve entities of shadow and corruption. They know enough ritual magic to be dangerous — their prayers can manifest as shadowy attacks. They seek artifacts of power for their patron and will sacrifice anything, including themselves, for their cause.

mercenary_veteran.name: mercenary veteran
mercenary_veteran.lore: Mercenary veterans are former soldiers who have survived too many campaigns. They have no political allegiance — they serve whoever pays, currently some dungeon power. Their combat technique is honed by decades of professional violence, making them far more dangerous than their simple equipment suggests.

battle_mage.name: battle mage
battle_mage.lore: Battle mages specialize in combat-oriented magic, sacrificing breadth of spellcasting for power and speed. They typically wear light armor and rely on keeping distance from melee. The arcane tattoos covering their arms are magical conduits that accelerate spellcasting at the cost of significant pain.

hill_giant.name: hill giant
hill_giant.lore: Hill giants are the most common giant variety, dull-witted but immensely strong. They build crude dens in cavern systems and eat anything they can catch, including other giants if sufficiently hungry. Their hoards are eclectic collections of things they found interesting — shiny, large, or edible.

stone_giant.name: stone giant
stone_giant.lore: Stone giants dwell in the deepest caverns, their gray skin nearly indistinguishable from dungeon walls. They are far more intelligent than hill giants and have developed a sophisticated culture centered on art carved into cave walls. They view the surface world with contempt and surface-dwellers who venture below as trespassers.

cloud_giant.name: cloud giant
cloud_giant.lore: Cloud giants float between the material and elemental planes, their castles built on impossible storm-clouds. Those found in the deep dungeon are typically exiles or adventurers of their own kind, seeking ancient artifacts. They are proud to the point of arrogance, finding most humanoids too small to be worth killing.

storm_giant.name: storm giant
storm_giant.lore: Storm giants are among the most powerful mortal beings in existence, their power rivaling minor deities. They perceive the world on a geological timescale — they remember when the dungeon was first built. Their purposes are inscrutable to shorter-lived beings, but when they act, the consequences are felt for generations.

cyclops_brute.name: cyclops brute
cyclops_brute.lore: Cyclops brutes are larger and more violent relatives of the mythological cyclops, stripped of any shepherd's gentleness. Their single eye cannot gauge depth well, making them poor archers but devastating in melee where they can feel their target. They collect sheep-sized livestock or humanoids with equal indifference.

two_headed_ogre.name: two-headed ogre
two_headed_ogre.lore: Two-headed ogres are the result of a rare magical mutation — or perhaps a divine punishment. Their two heads argue constantly about tactics, food, and everything else, but in combat their four-armed style of attack is brutally effective. They can attack two targets simultaneously, which makes fighting them with a partner especially hazardous.

ice_troll.name: ice troll
ice_troll.lore: Ice trolls haunt the frozen sub-levels and flooded caverns that connect to glacial systems. Their regeneration is slowed by fire and stopped completely by salt, but in their icy domains they are nearly unkillable. Their saliva freezes on contact with warm flesh.

drow_warrior.name: drow warrior
drow_warrior.lore: Drow warriors are trained from birth in the fighting arts of the Underdark. Their equipment is of exquisite quality, though much of it crumbles in surface air. They favor poison-laced crossbow bolts to disable opponents for capture — drow prefer prisoners over corpses when possible.

drow_mage.name: drow mage
drow_mage.lore: Drow mages are among the most accomplished arcane practitioners in the deep dungeon. They specialize in shadow magic and illusions, disciplines that evolved in a lightless environment where shadows have more reality than light. Their spellbooks, if recoverable, are worth significant sums to surface scholars.

drow_priestess.name: drow priestess
drow_priestess.lore: Drow priestesses serve the Spider Queen and hold absolute authority in drow society above all but the Matron Mothers. They wield divine magic of terrifying efficacy, particularly spells of darkness, poison, and domination. Their favor raises the status of entire families; their displeasure ends bloodlines.

minotaur_warrior.name: minotaur warrior
minotaur_warrior.lore: Minotaur warriors are organized fighters who have built a martial culture around labyrinth warfare. They never become disoriented in maze-like environments and use this advantage ruthlessly. A minotaur warrior in its home territory is three times as dangerous as one encountered in open ground.

young_black_dragon.name: young black dragon
young_black_dragon.lore: Black dragons are among the most vile of dragonkind — cruel, sadistic, and petty. They lair in swamps and flooded dungeon sections, where their acid breath is most effective. Even young, they are extraordinarily dangerous and already beginning to plot the accumulation of a hoard worthy of their ego.

young_red_dragon.name: young red dragon
young_red_dragon.lore: Red dragons are the most arrogant of dragonkind, believing themselves superior to all other beings. Even a young red dragon has a fully developed ego and expects tribute. Their fire breath is the hottest of all dragon breath weapons — it can melt stone if sustained. Red dragon lairs are always notably warmer than surrounding areas.

adult_blue_dragon.name: adult blue dragon
adult_blue_dragon.lore: Adult blue dragons are fascinated by organization and order — not law in a moral sense, but perfect systemic arrangement. They maintain extensive networks of humanoid servants and are excellent strategists. Their lightning breath can chain between multiple targets, making them especially deadly against groups.

adult_red_dragon.name: adult red dragon
adult_red_dragon.lore: Adult red dragons are among the most feared creatures in existence. Their personality is a perfect storm of greed, pride, and cruelty. An adult red dragon's hoard is legendary — accumulated over centuries of systematic raiding and tribute-taking. They consider themselves the rightful rulers of whatever territory they claim.

drake_fire.name: fire drake
drake_fire.lore: Fire drakes are lesser kin to true dragons, possessing a fraction of their cousin's power and none of their intelligence. They operate on instinct — hunt, eat, hoard. Their fire spit is considerably less devastating than a dragon's breath but sufficient to ignite most dungeon materials. They are sometimes found as guards in more powerful monsters' lairs.

drake_cold.name: frost drake
drake_cold.lore: Frost drakes haunt the glacier-touched lower dungeon levels, their scales perpetually rime-covered. They ambush from above, coating prey with freezing breath before descending to feed. Their lairs are always recognizable by the icy coating on surrounding walls and the frozen remains of previous meals.

yuan_ti_pureblood.name: yuan-ti pureblood
yuan_ti_pureblood.lore: Yuan-ti are serpent-human hybrids who worship a dead serpent god whose demise did not diminish their devotion. Purebloods appear almost entirely human, their serpentine nature revealed only by slightly scaled patches of skin. They infiltrate human settlements and dungeon exploration parties as spies for the yuan-ti collective.

wyvern_greater.name: greater wyvern
wyvern_greater.lore: Greater wyverns are among the largest flying predators in the dungeon, their wingspan capable of blocking corridor exits. Unlike true dragons, they have no fire breath — they rely on their savage claws and an enormously powerful stinger at the tail's tip. Their venom is among the most potent naturally occurring toxins.

naga_guardian.name: naga guardian
naga_guardian.lore: Nagas are powerful serpentine beings of vast intelligence and magical ability. Guardian nagas in particular have been assigned permanent watch over specific locations — tombs, vaults, shrines — and have carried out this duty for centuries. They know everything about their assigned territory and find intruders personally offensive.
